title: Mastering Side Effects with the useEffect Hook

questions.sentence: In React a side effect is anything that happens outside the scope of rendering a component such as fetching data from an API manually updating the DOM or setting up a subscription
The useEffect hook is the standard way to handle these effects in functional components
Effectively using useEffect requires a deep understanding of its dependency array
If the array is empty the effect runs only once after the initial mount
Failing to manage dependencies correctly can lead to performance issues or infinite loops
Furthermore you must often return a cleanup function to prevent memory leaks such as clearing a timer or unsubscribing from a service
